100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 2, 1926, the British Empire celebrated the opening of the Empire Exhibition in Glasgow, showcasing the industrial and cultural achievements of its nations. In Mexico, President Plutarco Elías Calles continued his crackdown on the Catholic Church, intensifying tensions during the Cristero War. Meanwhile, in the United States, a major heatwave gripped the Midwest, raising concerns over the welfare of residents and crops alike.
Updated: Tuesday, June 2, 2026 at 10:48:04 AM CDT
